steps.title: Translate the time used per box
steps.content: $x$ large boxes use $10x$ minutes (10 min each). $y$ small boxes use $5y$ minutes (5 min each).

steps.title: Set the total equal to 700
steps.content: The total daily time is 700 minutes: $10x + 5y = 700$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• $5x+10y=700$: $5x + 10y = 700$: swaps the per-box times (5 min for large, 10 min for small).
  • $10x+5y=700$: $10x + 5y = 700$: correctly multiplies each box count by its own time.
  • $\left(x+y\right)\left(10+5\right)=700$: $(x+y)(10+5)=700$: this would only work if every box took 15 minutes, which is not the case.
  • $\left(10+x\right)\left(5+y\right)=700$: $(10+x)(5+y)=700$: adds counts and times, which is not how time accumulates.
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
